subject: Add HTTPS support between SOL003 Adapter and ETSI
commit_message:
Add HTTPS support between SOL003 Adapter and ETSI

Catalog Manager

Change-Id: I66c9cae7b583fa929c5ebe98731dd10616c07047
Issue-ID: SO-2752
Signed-off-by: waqas.ikram <waqas.ikram@est.tech>

Patch Set 3:

Well, the root issue is that some certificates are hardcoded (and they shouldn't).

With the aaf init container, we can retrieve the root CA AFAIK.
So, we could retrieve it and mount it in /usr/share/certificates (in memory) for the main container no?

@Krzysztof, what do you think?

Patch Set 3:

@Sylvain, @Krzysztof - As you know, we are following the current SO security mechanism. There was existing onap-ca.crt in the docker-files/ca-certificates directory, and we are adding mbs-ca.crt for MSB secure communication. 
As you said, we need a better solution for SO and other ONAP components, and asked the direction to SECCOM. I don't think that happens for Frankfurt. It is too late. I think we need to wait for Guilin and we all need to discuss a proper security solution for Guilin.
In my opinion, we should follow the current SO security mechanism and enhance it in the Guilin after SECCOM direction.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

My take on this is clear:

*No hard coding certificates in docker images in any case.* 

It's basically the worst thing that you can do.

I understand that this is the current state in SO but:
1) it doesn't mean that I accept it (I don't but I just don't have enough cycles to fix all bad constructions in ONAP code base at once)
2) It doesn't mean that you have to make things even worse by adding yet another hard coded certificate

As Sylvain said, the prefer way would be to use AAF init container. If you get a waiver from seccom/tsc we can discuss adding the certificate to OOM repo but never ever to the docker image.

Patch Set 3:

I think Brian confirmed, https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/so/+/102978/ start-app.sh (non root-user) should be fixed first regardless this bug. It broke the existing onap-ca.crt handling. 
Secondly, the MSB issued the self-signed certificate according to Huabing (MSB PTL). We got the MSB certificates from him, and he pointed the user story that MSB has a migration path for AAF in Guilin.  I don't know their story with TSC/SECCOM.
Thirdly, the security solution in ONAP is not yet production-qualify in my opinion. So, we are doing based on what we have. If our code is not acceptable, we need to treat the communication between the SOL003 Adapter and ETSI Catalog as an internal communication. But I hope we want to make the communication secure, so in Guilin, we know what to challenge. Not only this, the SOL003 Adapter and external SVNFM communication could be much complicated - we put this one as the Guilin work item.
